summary.intent: Harden the Moltis web application's remote access security surface, starting from a GitHub issue about disabling the terminal on public deployments, then progressively fixing all discovered security vulnerabilities.
summary.outcome: Implemented 4 commits of security hardening: terminal disable feature (config + env var), brute-force IP ban + account lockout, 9-item hardening pass (HSTS, session cap, HMAC-SHA256 API keys, password min 12, audit log, scope enforcement), and a second-pass fixing WS scope bypass, timing side-channel, setup code entropy, and startup TLS warning.

repo: Config is structured with top-level sections: [server], [tools.web], etc. — WebConfig lives under [tools.web], not a top-level [web]
Gon pattern is used to inject server-side config into the frontend JS (e.g. terminal_enabled, voice_enabled, graphql_enabled)
build_schema_map() in validate.rs must be updated whenever new config fields are added to avoid 'unknown field' warnings
Env vars like MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED follow the pattern of MOLTIS_VERSION for deployment-level overrides that bypass the config editor
WebSocket RPC dispatch uses authorize_method + MethodContext::client_scopes for per-method scope enforcement — scopes ARE enforced at RPC level
The beads CLI is used for issue tracking: pull beads, create issue, claim issue, close issue workflow
CUDA crates cause build failures on macOS — clippy and tests must be run with --exclude flags for those crates
unsafe {} blocks are fully denied by lint in this codebase — std::env::set_var/remove_var cannot be used in tests
Two build_gateway_base_internal functions exist (cfg-gated) — both need updating when AuthState changes
AuthIdentity is inserted into request extensions by auth_gate middleware and readable by subsequent handlers

code.finding: Test calling is_terminal_enabled() was fragile — reads MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED env var which could be set in CI; fixed to test field directly
WS handler tracks api_key_scopes and passes them via MethodContext, but header-based bearer API key auth discarded scopes — fixed by returning AuthIdentity from websocket_header_authenticated
resolve_client_ip was private — needed to be made pub to share with login_guard.rs
verify_password returned immediately when no password set (~0ms vs ~100ms with Argon2), leaking password existence via timing; fixed with DUMMY_ARGON2_HASH constant
AuthIdentity struct needed scopes field added; all construction sites required updating
require_config_access() needed AuthIdentity parameter to enforce operator.admin scope on config_save and restart — axum Extension<AuthIdentity> extractor used to pass identity
Setup code was 6 numeric digits (~900K combinations, brute-forceable in minutes); upgraded to 8 alphanumeric characters

workflow: axum Extension<T> extractor reads from request.extensions() — same map that extensions_mut().insert(T) writes to
When axum handler state type doesn't implement FromRef<S> for a needed type, Extension<T> extractors are the workaround
Cargo workspace deps (hmac, sha2) need to be explicitly added to crate-level Cargo.toml even if defined at workspace level
SQLite migrations are date-prefixed files in crates/gateway/migrations/ — test init methods also need manual schema updates
Playwright E2E tests run against a live server — testing gon-gated features requires either config file manipulation or client-side gon overrides

friction: CUDA crates cause persistent build failures on macOS, requiring --exclude flags on every clippy/test invocation
unsafe{} lint denial makes env var testing impossible — had to remove the env var override test entirely
scope enforcement on config routes required multiple approaches before finding a working pattern (RequireAdmin extractor failed due to FromRef bounds; fell back to Extension<AuthIdentity>)
require_config_access() refactor was messy — the function didn't have access to request extensions, requiring signature changes and multiple edit attempts
tools_routes.rs scope enforcement went through ~6 failed approaches before settling on Extension<AuthIdentity> pattern
cleanup test in login_guard failed because the cleanup logic kept IPs with consecutive_failures > 0 — required adding last_failure_at staleness tracking
password length test failures in auth_middleware integration tests after raising minimum to 12 chars — required updating hardcoded test passwords
open_items: Per-message rate limiting on WebSocket RPC — expensive operations like agents.run can be spammed by authenticated users
CSP style-src 'unsafe-inline' remains due to Tailwind — needs build pipeline change to use nonce-based injection
Audit logging for password changes and passkey registration (currently only logs login and API key events)
Session binding to originating IP — deferred due to breaking mobile/VPN use cases
API key rotation mechanism — currently manual (create, migrate, revoke); no atomic rotation
auth.disabled = true config flag could be set via the TOML editor — could add startup warning similar to TLS warning
E2E spec for terminal-disabled state was added but tests gon override client-side rather than testing real server config path
